session.authorizeAudio: Allow the use of audio in sessions
session.name: Session name

home.intro: BⱯBEL RËVOLUㅏION is a platform for creating participatory stories. It is both an ever-evolving work, completed creations, and an interface for creating participatory sessions and scenarios. This site invites you to dive into a digital writing space where everyone can contribute and co-create in their own way (previous version: https://babel.utc.fr/).
home.introHighlight: Join an ongoing session or create and lead your own session, your
home.emptyMessage: Come on, try to write something!
home.animateSession: Are you a teacher, workshop facilitator, or simply a revolutionary at heart? If you want to lead your own BⱯBEL RËVOLUㅏION, contact the site administrators who will open an account for you and guide you through the adventure. Contact: Serge Bouchardon (serge.bouchardon@utc.fr) and Isabelle Cros (isabelle.CROS@univ-amu.fr)

footer.citation: An expression that, just a moment ago, was still alive and seemed even unshakable, could suddenly vanish: it disappeared with the situation that generated it and will one day testify as a fossil.
footer.citationInfo: Klemperer, LTI, the language of the Third Reich, 1947
footer.mainText: BⱯBEL RËVOLUㅏION is an interactive, collaborative, multilingual dystopian story. The original scenario invited us to reflect on the link between the degradation of biodiversity and the degradation of linguistic and cultural diversity.
footer.title: Babel Revolution

exampleGraph.text1: The graph appears with several interconnected nodes. Each node contains a title, text, and author, serving as an entry point to a message. Observe the whole, feel the connections, and discover the relationships between each message.
exampleGraph.text2: Click on a node to reveal the details: the author and the content of the message. Each interaction unveils a new step in understanding the exchanges that precede your intervention.
exampleGraph.text3: By clicking on a node, you can add your contribution. Write your own message, which will be added to the network, creating a new link or continuing the conversation.
exampleGraph.text4: Grab a node and move it to see the links react. Move the entire graph, reorganize your view, and play with the gravity that binds the messages together.
exampleGraph.text5: Use the zoom to enlarge or shrink your view of the graph. Whether you want to delve into the details of a specific section or get an overview, it's up to you to choose the angle that suits you.

ia.visionPlaceholder: Describe the role you want the AI to play in your scenario...
ia.notFullySupported: Some aspects of your vision may not be fully supported by the current platform.
ia.canTriggerNodes: Trigger nodes in response to player actions
ia.visionTooShort: AI vision must be at least 10 characters long
ia.noCapabilities: Select at least one AI capability
ia.canCensorDesc: The AI will automatically hide or redact player contributions that contain forbidden words or topics.
ia.canTriggerNodesDesc: The AI will publish a pre-written story node when a player's contribution matches a defined condition.
ia.canEndSessionDesc: The AI will trigger one of the scenario's endings when a specific condition is met.
ia.visionDesc: Describe the AI's role: what it should watch for, enforce, and how it should behave. This guides all AI capabilities.
ia.bannedWordsDesc: Words that will be automatically replaced with #### in player contributions. Case-insensitive, works with accented variants.
ia.triggerRulesDesc: When a player's contribution matches a condition, the AI publishes the pre-written node as a story event. Each rule fires at most once per session.
ia.triggerConditionDesc: Describe what the player must say or do to trigger this event. Be specific — the AI uses this to judge each contribution.
ia.triggerNodeDesc: The content of the event node that will appear in the story graph when this rule fires.
ia.triggerSideDesc: Which side this event will be visible to.
ia.requiresFiredDesc: This rule will only activate after the selected rules have already fired.
ia.endConditionDesc: The AI ends the session when a player's contribution matches this condition.
ia.endConditionFieldDesc: Describe what must happen for the session to end. Be specific.
ia.endTitleDesc: Which ending to display when the condition is met.
